title: solanum dulcamara f. rupestre
intro: Solanum dulcamara f. rupestre, also known as the climbing nightshade, is a subspecies of the Solanum dulcamara plant, a flowering perennial native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and soothing digestive issues, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. Solanum dulcamara f. rupestre can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
